title: Turkey Fajita Fried Rice Bowls
description: Vegetable fried rice topped with seasoned lean ground turkey, tomato, cheese, and a Greek yogurt garnish
ingredients: 160g Roma tomato, diced (added at the bowl, not cooked in)
instructions: Bring 2 1/2 cups water and 1/4 tsp salt to a boil in a medium pot.
Add 1 1/2 cups basmati rice, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook 12-14 minutes until liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat, keep covered.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large non-stick pan over medium heat. Add 500g lean ground turkey, breaking it apart, and cook until fully browned and internal temperature reaches 74°C/165°F.

Once turkey is cooked, add 1/4 cup water, 1 tsp garlic salt, and the seasoning mix: 1 1/2 tsp chili powder, 1 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1 1/2 tsp onion powder, 3/4 tsp crushed red pepper flakes, 3/4 tsp dried oregano, 1 tbsp paprika, 2 1/4 tsp ground cumin, 3/4 tsp sea salt, 3/4 tsp black pepper, 1/8 tsp cinnamon, and 1/8 tsp cloves. Stir to coat.
Keep over medium heat just until fragrant, then remove turkey from the pan and set aside — it does not go back into the pan.
In the same pan, increase heat to medium-high. Add 320g sliced bell pepper, 1 diced medium zucchini, 113g diced onion, and 1 tsp garlic salt. Cook 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in 2 chopped green onions and cook 1 minute, then add the cooked rice and stir-fry together over medium-high heat for 1-2 minutes to combine.
Divide the vegetable fried rice among 4 bowls. Top each bowl individually with turkey, 160g diced Roma tomato (divided across bowls), and 1 cup shredded Monterey Jack cheese (divided across bowls).
Finish with 6 tbsp 0% Greek yogurt (about 1 1/2 tbsp per bowl) and 2 tbsp lime juice (or juice of 1 lime).
notes: Without cheese: 538 cal, 35g protein, 69g carb, 14g fat per serving.
